title: C++ Quiz
questions:
24. What is the value of sizeof('a') in C++?
A) Always 1
B) Same as sizeof(int) (which might be 4)
C) Depends on character encoding (can be 2)
D) Compiler-dependent value without specifications

28. Array references can look bizarre. What is printed?
int arr[] = {10, 20, 30};
std::cout << 2[arr];
A) Compile Error
B) Memory Address
C) 30
D) Undefined Behavior

31. What is the output of this ternary operator type match challenge?
std::cout << (true ? 1 : 2.0);
A) Prints integer 1
B) Prints floating point 1.0
C) Compilation Error due to conflicting return types
D) Undefined evaluation

34. Macro definitions substitute text directly. What is the value of result?
#define MULTIPLY(a, b) a * b
int result = MULTIPLY(2 + 3, 4);
A) 20
B) 14
C) 24
D) Compile Error

36. What is the risk or output of using an initialized constant reference?
const int& ref = 42;
std::cout << ref;
A) Compile error: cannot bind non-const reference to temporary
B) Undefined behavior because 42 is destroyed immediately
C) Perfectly valid, prints 42 (extends lifetime)
D) Prints memory address of the temporary constant

38. Signed overflow is a major trap in C++. What is the result?
int max_int = 2147483647;
std::cout << (max_int + 1 < max_int);
A) Outputs 1 (True)
B) Outputs 0 (False)
C) Undefined Behavior
D) Compile Error

40. What does this declaration do in modern C++?
int a{};
std::cout << a;
A) Prints garbage value because it's uninitialized
B) Prints 0 due to value-initialization
C) Compilation Error
D) Throws a runtime initialization exception
